question: Do you share our data with third parties? Who are your sub-processors? Where are they located?
title: Third-Party Data Sharing, Sub-processor Transparency, and Jurisdictional Control
bullets: Provide a complete and current list of sub-processors with name, purpose, data accessed, and geographic location
Explain data sharing boundaries (what data is shared, under what conditions, and for which services)
Describe contractual and technical controls (DPAs, SCCs, encryption, least-privilege access) ensuring equivalent protections
Detail governance for onboarding/changes (due diligence, risk assessments, customer notification/opt-out)
suggested_inputs: Sub-processor registry (URL or document) with version history
Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) and Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs)
Third-party risk assessment and due diligence reports
Access logs and diagrams showing data flows to each sub-processor

good.text: We maintain a complete, public sub-processor list including company name, service (e.g., hosting, logging), specific data accessed, and geographic location. The list is versioned and updated within 30 days of any change.

All sub-processors are bound by DPAs with equivalent protections (including SCCs where applicable). Data is shared on a least-privilege basis, encrypted in transit/at rest, and access is audited. We can provide DPA summaries and evidence of controls.

We share data only with specific providers (e.g., cloud hosting, monitoring). Data residency is enforced so processing remains within selected regions/jurisdictions. Cross-border transfers are controlled and documented.

New sub-processors undergo formal due diligence (security, privacy, legal). Customers are notified in advance with an objection window. We support alternatives (e.g., regional/on-prem options) where feasible.

partial.text: We use several third-party providers and can share a list on request, but details on data accessed, locations, or update cadence are limited.
Our partners meet high security standards, though specific contractual controls and verification processes are not fully disclosed.
Data may be shared with service providers for operations, but the exact scope, categories of data, and purposes are not clearly defined.
We may update sub-processors over time, but customer notification timelines and objection rights are not clearly specified.

bad.text: We use industry-standard partners without listing who they are or what they do.
We cannot disclose our partners for competitive reasons.
We do not use third parties, despite relying on cloud providers for hosting and operations.
Our partners are secure, but we do not provide evidence of due diligence or contractual protections.

follow_ups: Provide a complete sub-processor list including company name, service provided, data accessed, and geographic location.
Share DPA details for each sub-processor—are protections equivalent to our contract with you?
Describe your due diligence and risk assessment process before onboarding a new sub-processor.
If a sub-processor experiences a breach, what is your notification timeline and containment process?
Can we restrict specific sub-processors or require regional/on-prem alternatives?
